evaluation.manager_summary: This intake call with Sophia, who disclosed daily cocaine use for two years, contains serious compliance violations including multiple treatment outcome guarantees and promises. The agent failed to build rapport, missed critical clinical screening questions (last use, withdrawal, safety), did not assess urgency appropriately for daily stimulant use, and provided vague next steps despite the caller requesting specificity. Immediate coaching required on compliance language and clinical triage protocols.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: The agent failed to acknowledge Sophia's emotional state or the difficulty of her situation in the critical first 60 seconds. Instead of building rapport, the agent immediately moved to a transactional question ('How long has this been going on?') without any warmth, acknowledgment, or use of the caller's name.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Start by acknowledging the caller's courage in reaching out and their expressed worry before jumping into intake questions—use their name and match their emotional register.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: The agent captured carrier name, member ID, and group number correctly. However, the agent did not explain the verification process or frame it as a step toward help, and the follow-up on benefits was vague ('we'll verify your benefits and let you know').
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Frame insurance verification as part of the pathway to help, and explain when and how the caller will receive benefit details.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: The agent captured substance type (cocaine), duration (two years), and frequency (daily), and asked about prior treatment. However, critical severity indicators were missing: no questions about last use, withdrawal symptoms, or co-occurring mental health concerns.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Always ask about last use, withdrawal symptoms, and whether there are any mental health concerns to properly assess urgency and treatment needs.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Daily cocaine use for two years represents a significant urgency signal that the agent did not adequately explore. The agent failed to ask about withdrawal risk, last use, or current safety, and treated this as a routine intake rather than assessing for immediate medical needs.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Daily stimulant use requires questions about last use, withdrawal concerns, and current safety to determine if same-day or this-week admission is needed.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Not applicable—the caller is the patient herself, as confirmed when she says 'I've been struggling with cocaine use' and 'I'm worried about me too.'

When the caller expressed concern about cost and insurance coverage, the agent offered only a vague reassurance ('we'll get you taken care of') without providing concrete alternatives, explaining the verification timeline, or offering financing/payment options.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: When cost concerns arise, acknowledge them specifically and offer concrete information about financing options, payment plans, or what the verification process will reveal.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: The next step was extremely vague ('someone from our admissions team will call you. Probably in the next day or so'). No specific timing, no clear ownership, and the agent deflected when the caller asked for specificity. The caller had to ask what happens next.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Always provide a specific callback window (e.g., 'by 2pm tomorrow'), confirm who will call, and state what the caller should expect in that conversation.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: The agent made multiple serious compliance violations: guaranteeing treatment outcomes ('our 30-day program gets people better,' 'You'll come out of here a completely different person,' 'this time will be different, I promise'), and claiming success rates without qualification. These constitute outcome guarantees and potentially unauthorized practice claims.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Never guarantee treatment outcomes or promise results—use language like 'our program is designed to support recovery' and avoid diagnostic or outcome-based promises entirely.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I understand. How long has this been going on?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Hi. I'm Sophia. I'm calling because I've been struggling with cocaine use and my family is really worried about me. I'm worried about me too.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. We have a really great program here and I can tell you — our 30-day program gets people better.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: We'll verify your benefits and let you know. Don't worry, we'll get you taken care of.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: About two years. It's daily now.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Great. And have you had any previous treatment?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I did a 30-day residential about three years ago.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: About two years. It's daily now.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Someone from our admissions team will call you. Probably in the next day or so.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Hi. I'm Sophia. I'm calling because I've been struggling with cocaine use and my family is really worried about me. I'm worried about me too.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: A little. I don't know what insurance covers.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: We'll verify your benefits and let you know. Don't worry, we'll get you taken care of.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Someone from our admissions team will call you. Probably in the next day or so.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Do you have a more specific time?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'll put you on the list. You'll hear from us.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. We have a really great program here and I can tell you — our 30-day program gets people better. You'll come out of here a completely different person.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: And you're back to using — okay. Well this time will be different, I promise. Our program has an excellent success rate.
